revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] {{italic title}} {{short description|Traditional Japanese fortune-telling method}}
{{nihongo|'''''Tengenjutsu'''''|天源術|lead=yes}} is a Japanese [[:en:fortune telling|fortune telling]] method. It is based on [[:en:Yin and yang|Yin and yang]] and the [[:en:Five elements (Japanese philosophy)|five elements]], and uses a person's birth date in the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]] and physical appearance to predict one's fate. ''Tengenjutsu'' originated in various [[:en:Taoism in Japan|Chinese practices]], but was first systemized by the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] monk [[:en:Tenkai|Tenkai]]. It is also the origin of ''[[:en:Tōkyūjutsu|Tōkyūjutsu]]''.<ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Digital [[Daijisen]] | title = 天源術 | url = http://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%90%E8%A1%93 | accessdate = 2009-12-28 | language = Japanese | publisher = [[Shogakukan]] }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = [[Daijirin]] | title = てんげんじゅつ 【天源術】 | url = http://dictionary.goo.ne.jp/leaf/jn/134781/m0u// | accessdate = 2009-12-28 | language = Japanese | edition = 2 | publisher = [[Sanseido]] }} [http://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%90%E8%A1%93 Alt. link]. </ref><ref name="Sawada">{{cite book|last=Sawada|first=Janine Anderson Tasca|title=Practical pursuits: religion, politics, and personal cultivation in nineteenth-century Japan|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|year=2004|page=30|isbn=0-8248-2752-X|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_5bQxGr6IHEC&pg=PA30|accessdate=2009-12-20}}</ref>
== See also == * [[:en:Futomani|Futomani]] * [[Itako]] * [[:en:Kokkuri|Kokkuri]] * [[:en:O-mikuji|O-mikuji]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[:en:Sanpaku|Sanpaku]]
== References == {{reflist}}]]{{Japan-reli-stub}}

{{nihongo|'''''Tengenjutsu'''''|天源術|lead=yes}} is a Japanese [[:en:fortune telling|fortune telling]] method. It is based on [[:en:Yin and yang|Yin and yang]] and the [[:en:Five elements (Japanese philosophy)|five elements]], and uses a person's birth date in the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]] and physical appearance to predict one's fate. ''Tengenjutsu'' originated in various [[:en:Taoism in Japan|Chinese practices]], but was first systemized by the early [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] monk [[:en:Tenkai|Tenkai]]. It is also the origin of ''[[:en:Tōkyūjutsu|Tōkyūjutsu]]''.<ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Digital [[Daijisen]] | title = 天源術 | url = http://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%90%E8%A1%93 | accessdate = 2009-12-28 | language = Japanese | publisher = [[Shogakukan]] }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = [[Daijirin]] | title = てんげんじゅつ 【天源術】 | url = http://dictionary.goo.ne.jp/leaf/jn/134781/m0u// | accessdate = 2009-12-28 | language = Japanese | edition = 2 | publisher = [[Sanseido]] }} [http://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%90%E8%A1%93 Alt. link]. </ref><ref name="Sawada">{{cite book|last=Sawada|first=Janine Anderson Tasca|title=Practical pursuits: religion, politics, and personal cultivation in nineteenth-century Japan|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|year=2004|page=30|isbn=0-8248-2752-X|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_5bQxGr6IHEC&pg=PA30|accessdate=2009-12-20}}</ref>

It is also the origin of ''[[:en:Tōkyūjutsu|Tōkyūjutsu]]''.<ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Digital [[Daijisen]] | title = 天源術 | url = http://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%90%E8%A1%93 | accessdate = 2009-12-28 | language = Japanese | publisher = [[Shogakukan]] }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = [[Daijirin]] | title = てんげんじゅつ 【天源術】 | url = http://dictionary.goo.ne.jp/leaf/jn/134781/m0u// | accessdate = 2009-12-28 | language = Japanese | edition = 2 | publisher = [[Sanseido]] }} [http://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%90%E8%A1%93 Alt. link]. </ref><ref name="Sawada">{{cite book|last=Sawada|first=Janine Anderson Tasca|title=Practical pursuits: religion, politics, and personal cultivation in nineteenth-century Japan|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|year=2004|page=30|isbn=0-8248-2752-X|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_5bQxGr6IHEC&pg=PA30|accessdate=2009-12-20}}</ref>

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] {{italic title}} {{short description|Traditional Japanese fortune-telling method}}
{{nihongo|'''''Tengenjutsu'''''|天源術|lead=yes}} is a Japanese [[:en:fortune telling|fortune telling]] method. It is based on {{ill|Yin and yang|en|Yin and yang|WD=Q62744|lt=Yin and yang}} and the [[:en:Five elements (Japanese philosophy)|five elements]], and uses a person's birth date in the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|en|Sexagenary cycle|WD=Q246658|lt=Sexagenary cycle}} and physical appearance to predict one's fate. ''Tengenjutsu'' originated in various {{ill|Taoism in Japan|en|Taoism in Japan|WD=Q7684269|lt=Chinese practices}}, but was first systemized by the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} monk {{ill|Tenkai|en|Tenkai|WD=Q1142310|lt=Tenkai}}. It is also the origin of ''{{ill|Tōkyūjutsu|en|Tōkyūjutsu|WD=Q11559755|lt=Tōkyūjutsu}}''.<ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Digital [[Daijisen]] | title = 天源術 | url = http://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%90%E8%A1%93 | accessdate = 2009-12-28 | language = Japanese | publisher = [[Shogakukan]] }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = [[Daijirin]] | title = てんげんじゅつ 【天源術】 | url = http://dictionary.goo.ne.jp/leaf/jn/134781/m0u// | accessdate = 2009-12-28 | language = Japanese | edition = 2 | publisher = [[Sanseido]] }} [http://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%90%E8%A1%93 Alt. link]. </ref><ref name="Sawada">{{cite book|last=Sawada|first=Janine Anderson Tasca|title=Practical pursuits: religion, politics, and personal cultivation in nineteenth-century Japan|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|year=2004|page=30|isbn=0-8248-2752-X|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_5bQxGr6IHEC&pg=PA30|accessdate=2009-12-20}}</ref>
== See also == * {{ill|Futomani|en|Futomani|WD=Q5510530|lt=Futomani}} * [[Itako]] * {{ill|Kokkuri|en|Kokkuri|WD=Q17050363|lt=Kokkuri}} * {{ill|O-mikuji|en|O-mikuji|WD=Q1069592|lt=O-mikuji}} * [[Onmyōdō]] * {{ill|Sanpaku|en|Sanpaku|WD=Q1815087|lt=Sanpaku}}
== References == {{reflist}}]]{{Japan-reli-stub}}

''Tengenjutsu'' originated in various {{ill|Taoism in Japan|en|Taoism in Japan|WD=Q7684269|lt=Chinese practices}}, but was first systemized by the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} monk {{ill|Tenkai|en|Tenkai|WD=Q1142310|lt=Tenkai}}. ''Tengenjutsu'' originated in various {{ill|Taoism in Japan|en|Taoism in Japan|WD=Q7684269|lt=Chinese practices|1=Taoism in Japan}}, but was first systemized by the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} monk {{ill|Tenkai|en|Tenkai|WD=Q1142310|lt=Tenkai|1=Tenkai}}. It is also the origin of ''{{ill|Tōkyūjutsu|en|Tōkyūjutsu|WD=Q11559755|lt=Tōkyūjutsu|1=Tōkyūjutsu}}''.<ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Digital [[Daijisen]] | title = 天源術 | url = http://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%90%E8%A1%93 | accessdate = 2009-12-28 | language = Japanese | publisher = [[Shogakukan]] }}</ref><ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = [[Daijirin]] | title = てんげんじゅつ 【天源術】 | url = http://dictionary.goo.ne.jp/leaf/jn/134781/m0u// | accessdate = 2009-12-28 | language = Japanese | edition = 2 | publisher = [[Sanseido]] }} [http://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E6%BA%90%E8%A1%93 Alt. link]. </ref><ref name="Sawada">{{cite book|last=Sawada|first=Janine Anderson Tasca|title=Practical pursuits: religion, politics, and personal cultivation in nineteenth-century Japan|publisher=University of Hawai'i Press|year=2004|page=30|isbn=0-8248-2752-X|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_5bQxGr6IHEC&pg=PA30|accessdate=2009-12-20}}</ref>
